county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Monticello. Created 1834 from Carroll County. White County Historical Society. Tippecanoe River and Big Monon Creek valley settlements. Lake Shafer and Lake Freeman (Norway and Oakdale dams) area. BLM-GLO federal land patents. Monon and Brookston area records. Strong Methodist and Baptist church records. Agricultural county with good record survival. Check Carroll County for pre-1834 records. Monticello was named for Thomas Jefferson's home.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1834. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Indiana deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — earliest deeds may reference BLM-GLO original patents.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage applications, licenses, and returns from 1834. Indiana County Clerk issues marriage licenses. Applications include ages, birthplaces, and parents' names (improving detail over time).

Wills, administrations, guardianships, and estate inventories from 1834. Indiana probate is handled by the Circuit Court (County Clerk). Inventories often list personal property in detail and name heirs.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved at county level

source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original federal land patents for White County. Indiana is a federal land state — most land was sold through federal land offices. Patents record first purchaser from the government. Searchable online at glorecords.blm.gov. Cross-reference with county deed records for subsequent transfers.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Complete federal records — well-preserved and digitized at BLM-GLO

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Columbia City. Created 1835 from Allen and Huntington counties. Thomas R. Marshall (Woodrow Wilson's VP) was from Columbia City. Whitley County Historical Society. Blue River and Eel River valley settlements. BLM-GLO federal land patents. South Whitley, Churubusco, and Larwill area records. Strong Methodist and Lutheran church records. Check Allen and Huntington for pre-1835 records. Churubusco is famous for the 1949 'Beast of Busco' turtle hunt — community event records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1835. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Indiana deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — earliest deeds may reference BLM-GLO original patents.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage applications, licenses, and returns from 1835. Indiana County Clerk issues marriage licenses. Applications include ages, birthplaces, and parents' names (improving detail over time).

source_profiles.repository: Whitley County Clerk (Circuit/Superior Court), Columbia City
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, guardianships, and estate inventories from 1835. Indiana probate is handled by the Circuit Court (County Clerk). Inventories often list personal property in detail and name heirs.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved at county level

source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original federal land patents for Whitley County. Indiana is a federal land state — most land was sold through federal land offices. Patents record first purchaser from the government. Searchable online at glorecords.blm.gov. Cross-reference with county deed records for subsequent transfers.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Complete federal records — well-preserved and digitized at BLM-GLO
